questions.question: What two fields must ALWAYS appear in a Meridian vector search WHERE clause?
questions.explanation: Both user_id and workspace_id must be present. Omitting user_id could return other users' memories. Omitting workspace_id could return memories from other workspaces. Both scopes are required.

questions.question: What HTTP mechanism does Meridian use for AI streaming responses?
questions.explanation: SSE is used for AI streaming because it is server-to-client only (unidirectional), uses plain HTTP, and has native browser reconnect logic. WebSocket is bidirectional and used for Yjs document sync.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `LLMProvider` interface?
options: To cache AI responses for performance
To allow switching AI providers without changing route handlers
To rate limit AI API calls
questions.answer: To allow switching AI providers without changing route handlers
questions.explanation: The LLMProvider abstraction decouples route handlers from specific providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Ollama). The LLMRouter selects a provider at runtime. Changing providers requires no handler changes.

questions.question: What distance threshold does Meridian use to filter pgvector similarity results?
questions.explanation: distance < 0.3 (cosine distance) means only memories with ≥70% semantic similarity are included. Higher threshold returns more results with lower relevance.

questions.question: Describe the complete `writeMemory` UPSERT pattern and explain why UPSERT is used instead of INSERT.
questions.answer_key: INSERT INTO user_memory (user_id, workspace_id, memory_key, memory_value, embedding, tier) VALUES (...) ON CONFLICT (user_id, workspace_id, memory_key) DO UPDATE SET memory_value = EXCLUDED.memory_value, embedding = EXCLUDED.embedding, last_accessed_at = NOW(). UPSERT is used because calling writeMemory with the same key should update the existing record, not create a duplicate. INSERT alone would fail or create multiple records for the same memory key.
questions.rubric: Full credit: includes ON CONFLICT clause, updates all three fields (value, embedding, last_accessed_at), explains duplicate prevention.

questions.question: What does `X-Accel-Buffering: no` do, and why is it needed for SSE?
questions.answer_key: It disables Nginx's response buffering for this connection. Without it, Nginx buffers the full SSE stream before sending it to the client, defeating the streaming behavior — all tokens would arrive at once instead of incrementally. The header tells the proxy to pass bytes through immediately as they are written.
questions.rubric: Full credit: explains Nginx buffering AND the consequence (all tokens arrive at once). Partial: explains it disables buffering without the consequence.
